16â†’package com.ichi2.anki
17â†’
18â†’import android.content.Context
19â†’import android.content.Intent
20â†’import android.os.Bundle
21â†’import android.view.KeyEvent
22â†’import androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
23â†’import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentContainerView
24â†’import androidx.fragment.app.commit
25â†’import com.ichi2.themes.setTransparentStatusBar
26â†’import com.ichi2.utils.getInstanceFromClassName
27â†’import kotlin.reflect.KClass
28â†’import kotlin.reflect.jvm.jvmName
29â†’
30â†’/**
31â†’ * Activity aimed to host a fragment on the entire screen.
32â†’ * For that, it uses [R.layout.single_fragment_activity], which has only a [FragmentContainerView]
33â†’ *
34â†’ * Useful to avoid creating a Activity for every new screen
35â†’ * while being able to reuse the fragment on other places.
36â†’ *
37â†’ * [getIntent] can be used as an easy way to build a [SingleFragmentActivity]
38â†’ */
39â†’open class SingleFragmentActivity : AnkiActivity() {
40â†’    override fun onCreate(savedInstanceState: Bundle?) {
41â†’        if (showedActivityFailedScreen(savedInstanceState)) {
42â†’            return
43â†’        }
44â†’        super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)
45â†’        setContentView(R.layout.single_fragment_activity)
46â†’        setTransparentStatusBar()
47â†’
48â†’        // avoid recreating the fragment on configuration changes
49â†’        if (savedInstanceState != null) {
50â†’            return
51â†’        }
52â†’
53â†’        val fragmentClassName = requireNotNull(intent.getStringExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA)) {
54â†’            "'$FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA' extra should be provided"
55â†’        }
56â†’        val fragment = getInstanceFromClassName<Fragment>(fragmentClassName).apply {
57â†’            arguments = intent.getBundleExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA)
58â†’        }
59â†’        supportFragmentManager.commit {
60â†’            replace(R.id.fragment_container, fragment)
61â†’        }
62â†’    }
63â†’
64â†’    override fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean {
65â†’        val fragment = supportFragmentManager.findFragmentById(R.id.fragment_container)!!
66â†’        if (fragment is DispatchKeyEventListener && fragment.dispatchKeyEvent(event)) {
67â†’            return true
68â†’        }
69â†’        return super.dispatchKeyEvent(event)
70â†’    }
71â†’
72â†’    companion object {
73â†’        const val FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA = "fragmentName"
74â†’        const val FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA = "fragmentArgs"
75â†’
76â†’        fun getIntent(context: Context, fragmentClass: KClass<out Fragment>, arguments: Bundle? = null): Intent {
77â†’            return Intent(context, SingleFragmentActivity::class.java).apply {
78â†’                putExtra(FRAGMENT_NAME_EXTRA, fragmentClass.jvmName)
79â†’                putExtra(FRAGMENT_ARGS_EXTRA, arguments)
80â†’            }
81â†’        }
82â†’    }
83â†’}
84â†’
85â†’interface DispatchKeyEventListener {
86â†’    fun dispatchKeyEvent(event: KeyEvent): Boolean
87â†’}

202â†’                def outputFile = output.outputFile
